original_hypothesis.title: Missing Upper Bound Validation on liquidationRewardPercent and Other Fee Parameters
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: During initialization and updateConfig, liquidationRewardPercent, swapFeeAPR, and fragmentationFee have no upper bound validation. liquidationRewardPercent could be set > 100% (PERCENT), swapFeeAPR could be set to extreme values, and fragmentationFee has no cap. While the unit mismatch bug (TECH-H001) currently masks the reward issue, fixing that bug without adding bounds could create over-rewarding liquidators.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Initialize.validateInitializeFeeConfigParams explicitly marks liquidationRewardPercent, swapFeeAPR, and fragmentationFee as 'N/A' for validation. A malicious or careless admin could set liquidationRewardPercent to > 100%, meaning the liquidator gets more than the full collateral surplus.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deploy.sol sets liquidationRewardPercent=0.05e18 (5%), fragmentationFee=5e6, swapFeeAPR=0.005e18 — all reasonable, but no code prevents unreasonable values

proof_of_signal.pattern: Comments 'validate liquidationRewardPercent // N/A' and 'validate swapFeeAPR // N/A' — explicit skip of bounds checking

preconditions: Admin sets extreme fee parameter values

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Missing bounds validation on critical fee parameters
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: liquidationRewardPercent could be > PERCENT (100%), causing over-rewarding
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should validate liquidationRewardPercent <= PERCENT, and fragmentationFee within reasonable bounds

analysis_questions: What happens if liquidationRewardPercent > 100% with the unit bug fixed?
What is the worst-case impact of unbounded fragmentationFee?
Are there other fee parameters without bounds?
validation_requirements: Set liquidationRewardPercent > PERCENT and test liquidation
Set extreme fragmentationFee and test credit fractionalization
Verify all fee parameters have appropriate bounds

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Admin (or compromised admin key) sets liquidationRewardPercent to 200%. If the unit mismatch bug is later fixed, liquidators receive more collateral than the debt value plus surplus, potentially draining borrower collateral and creating unfair liquidations.
prerequisites: Admin action to set extreme parameters

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potential protocol misconfiguration leading to unfair liquidations or broken fee mechanics

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for extreme fee parameter values

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
